page_content:
CBMG - Cell Biology & Molecular Genetics

CBMG613 Microbiomes in Health, Disease, and Application (2 Credits) Investigation of microbiomes in human and animal health, disease, and industrial applications, with an emphasis on underlying functional mechanisms. No prior programming experience required.

CBMG626 Quantitative Modeling for Experimental Biologists (2 Credits) A toolkit for understanding processes through the generation of useful models constrained by data. Topics explored include Simulations, Information Theory, Dynamical Systems, and Control Theory. Prerequisite: BIOL705; or permission of the instructor .

Recommended: Some familiarity with programming (NetLogo, R and/or Python) is helpful.
